subject: UX & Research
title: Error Rate
description: A 1-minute de-risking session on Error Rate.

hook.text: Users hit the wrong button 40% of the time. They recover, but each error costs confidence and patience.

buildup.text: Error rate counts wrong actions per task. Slips are accidental; mistakes are intentional but wrong choices.

discovery.text: Slips need better affordances. Mistakes need clearer labels. The fix depends on the error type.

twist.text: Low error rate doesn't mean good UX. Users might avoid features entirely because they fear making errors.

climax.text: Track error recovery too. A form that catches typos instantly is better than one that fails on submit.

punchline.text: Slips need better controls. Mistakes need better labels. Know the type.

quiz.question: What is the difference between slips and mistakes in error classification?
options: Slips are accidental wrong actions; mistakes are intentional actions based on wrong understanding
Slips happen on mobile; mistakes happen on desktop
Slips are minor; mistakes are always critical
